message:
chore(beads): mark ft-7bcox and ft-zoxxq.2 in_progress to match landed work

Transitions two beads from open → in_progress to reflect the
companion code commits on this branch. Pure status sync — no
description, priority, dependency, comment, label, or close
metadata is touched, and no other beads change.

Bead changes:
- ft-7bcox ([LOW] FrameWriter Drop flush may abort on double-panic
  during unwind (7c8deb1d smell))
    open → in_progress
    updated_at: 2026-04-24T21:29:18Z → 2026-04-26T01:54:43Z
    Matches the catch_unwind-wrap fix landed in
    crates/frankenterm-core/src/recording.rs on this branch
    (commit "fix(recording): wrap FrameWriter::drop flush in
    catch_unwind to avoid double-panic abort"). The bead remains
    open rather than closed because the parallel agent that
    landed the fix opted to leave final closure for after the
    next CI cycle confirms the new
    frame_writer_drop_*_during_panic_unwind tests run green
    across the matrix.

- ft-zoxxq.2 ([ft-zoxxq.2] relocate WeztermClient + concrete
  types to mux_client.rs; leave wezterm.rs as re-export shim)
    open → in_progress
    updated_at: 2026-04-26T00:06:33Z → 2026-04-26T01:54:50Z
    Matches the first slice of the relocation landed in
    "refactor(frankenterm-core): ft-zoxxq.2 first slice —
    relocate PaneSize + CursorVisibility into mux_client" on
    this branch. The bead stays open because subsequent
    sub-slices (CwdInfo, PaneInfo, SpawnTarget, BackendKind,
    WeztermClient, the 5 impl MuxInterface for X blocks) still
    have to land before the relocation epic is complete.

Verification before commit:
- git diff -U0 -- .beads/issues.jsonl: only the two issues
  above appear in the patch.
- No close_reason, closed_at, dependencies, labels, or comment
  fields are inserted/removed/modified.
- Both transitions are open → in_progress; nothing transitions
  to closed or back to open.

Co-author note: beads timestamp/state mutations were emitted by
parallel agents during the same multi-agent work session;
committed verbatim per AGENTS.md's "treat parallel-agent changes
as your own" rule.
